Now this module is a fun and exciting one, but it's also an incredibly powerful part of the program. There you're going to be taught how to ethically spy on your competitors. It's the single most important factor in keeping a prospect on the straight line. Unfortunately, online we don't have the luxury of asking qualifying questions to our prospects, but we try to go forward in the sale. First, we need to gather as much intelligence on our process as we can, and we need to know exactly what makes them tick. The reason I say this is one of my favorite modules, if not my single favorite module, is because it ties in to everything else we've done. With respect to actually building your sales funnel, the best way to build your sales funnel is to look at what your competitors are doing, not only your competitors, but look for what the market wants. We have tools now, tools at our disposal, where we can actually determine or identify what it is the market wants, what they're searching for, what your ideal customer is asking suppliers for, they're asking you, telling you, guys, I want this, and we can actually just tap into that data and build an awesome sales funnel based on A, what they want, B, what your competitors are doing, and what's also great about ethically spying on your competitors and your market is it also helps us with our pricing decisions. For instance, we will know exactly what people are pricing particular products out, we'll know what's selling, what isn't selling, and we may want to come in a little lower than our competitors for our self-liquidating offer, which is the second step of our sales funnel, then we may want to come up a little higher than our competitors for our core offering, and that's because, of course, we will be able to convert at a much higher rate because we had already turned a prospect into a buyer, so we can therefore rely on some amount of market inertia to encourage that buyer to continue to buy from us, and our customers don't have that luxury because they didn't have a self-liquidating offer at the front of their funnel. It helps us with building our funnel, it helps us with traffic acquisition. We can find out where our competitors are getting their traffic. We can find out where they're getting their referral traffic. For instance, you could be on a content site, just a regular website or a blog or anything else, and you could find a link, it's on AnkerTechs, and there's just a link, a hyperlink, that you can click onto somewhere and it will land onto another page. Your competitors might be getting a lot of traffic through those content networks. We want to find out what websites are referring a lot of traffic to your competitors. When I say content networks, sure, there are those hyperlinks, but also there are banner ads on those content networks. We know that a lot of different content sites, excuse me, we know a lot of different sites that sell AdSense ads or they sell Ad Inventory or Space for Advertisements, Space for you to place a banner ad. What we can do is find out which sites are sending most of the traffic to our competitors. We can actually approach those sites directly and say, hey, why don't we do a deal and why don't you place some of my ads on your website directly as opposed to going through the network that they're using, like GDN or the Google Display Network, for example. We can find out just about anything, where they get their traffic, what changes that they've done to their website, how many websites your particular competitor owns. Your competitor may have a few different websites, and you may think it's only the one, but they may have a few different websites, and they're attracting traffic from all different kinds of places and putting them through all different kinds of funnels, and you are none the wiser. So we're going to leave no stone unturned. You can even find out things like who designed their logo or which supplier they're getting a particular product from. Sales funnel is incredibly important and you guys know the fundamentals of a sales funnel now because we've gone through it in great detail. You know that you're going to have in most cases the perfect SLM sales funnel which is an opt-in page followed by a self-liquidating offer page which is our low ticket offer. Then there's going to be followed by an order form if we're actually looking at the image but in practice, if we bypass the image, it's going to go straight into an upsell which is our core offering. That's the product that we intended to sell from the outset and that's our margin product. That's the product we really want to sell. That's the one that we're going to make the most amount of money from and then we're going to have a series of upsells and downsells that are of course optional. And then that's going to lead to a thank you page. You guys need to know the fundamentals of a sales funnel. You need to know how it works. You need to know why it works. You need to understand the psychology so that you can truly apply a lot of what we're going to be talking about when we say things like this is the sales funnel that our competitors have. This is what the market wants. The market is searching for this. Your market, whatever market you're in, you could be in the weight loss market. You could be in the training and education market. You can be in bricks and mortar store. It doesn't matter. It doesn't matter. So whatever market you're in, we're going to find out what exactly your market is searching for and if they're searching for it and there's sufficient demand for it, why don't you guys bolt it onto your funnel? Why don't you bolt it onto your funnel? So if you find out that your customers are searching for a particular item that's only a cheap low ticket item, then why not have that function as your self-liquidating offer? If you find out your market is really being forced for a really expensive high margin product, a product that's worth a whole lot of money and you can in turn make a decent margin from having acquired that, for having sold this particular product, then why not bolt it at the back of your funnel and have it listed as an upsell? So it doesn't necessarily need to be a core product, not telling you to change your business. You can leave your core offering exactly how it is and that's probably what you're selling right now if you actually do have a business and you're selling cool time now. And then you can bolt onto the back of it and also upsell and that upsell could be the particular product that we found out your market has an appetite for. So therefore the sales funnel module is ultra important so you guys can understand it and when you're talking about and when we talk about things like sales funnels, you guys are not going to know exactly what we're talking about. You can understand that, hey, I can bolt this at the end of my funnel. I didn't even think about that. And what happens when you keep on bolting things at the end of your funnel and adding little steps and things like that, especially at the end, at the beginning, the main thing about the funnel, the main value and the magic of the SLM funnel is actually converting a prospect into a buyer, right? Because the first two steps were not making much money. The opt-in were not making a dime at all because we're offering them something free and we're even paying for that traffic. And the next step, which is our soft liquidating offer, we're only making a small amount of money. We're not making much at all because it's only a low ticket product. So the first half of that funnel, the real magic, the power of it is actually converting prospects that don't even know you into customers. So once they become customers, now we're going to extract the most amount of value from them as possible. So the back end of the funnel from the core offering all the way to the your last upsell or downsell serves to increase the value of every single person that enters our funnel. We're going to find out who's giving you the most amount of issue, who's capturing most of your market. Because remember every business you're in, there's a market. Okay, it is a market. So no matter how unique you can, you're convinced your particular product or services and it may be unique and I hope it's unique. I really do hope you all have a USB. So something that kind of makes your product different from the competitors, something that makes it, you know, even a little edgier, it could be prettier, it could be simpler to use, it could be cheaper, it could be faster, it could be bigger, smaller, whatever. There needs to be some kind of a USB and provide and even though you do have a USB and I hope you all have a kick-ass USB, USB, of course, is a unique selling proposition or just something that differs you from your competitors. You're still in a market. There's a broader market. There's a market for a particular product. Even though yours is different, it still sits in a market. And the objective here is to find out who the market leaders are and to deconstruct their business, find out everything that it is that they're doing that's working for them. Do the things that are working for them, cut the things that aren't working for them and slowly through smart straight line marketing, we're going to etch our way into leadership. And hopefully, guys, your market is actually trending upwards and not downwards. Now, if your market is trending downwards, which means your market is actually getting smaller and fewer and fewer people are buying your particular product, that could be a cause for concern because unless you're a leader right now, if you're a leader in a market that's actually diminishing in size, then there may still be some value in your business. But if you're not a leader and you're a follower and the competitors are so far ahead of you and the market is continuing to decrease in size, then it's going to be very difficult to overtake that market leader because there's so much market inertia because of brands and the way people think and operate and the market's not growing and small. Then actually overtaking your competitors becomes a challenge. But if you're operating in a market that's actually growing, it's on an upward trend. So whatever business you're in, it's actually growing the actual market. Then what happens is as you continue to improve your straight line marketing skills and move an extra closer and closer into leadership, your personal growth or your business growth compounds with the market growth and you really drive into leadership and your business just grows. You won't be able to compute how fast you're actually going to grow and how big you're actually going to become because you're growing because of your skills, straight line marketers and straight line persuaders. And then also as whatever it is you do with respect to your core delivery, if you're a dog trainer, you have to be a really great dog trainer. So all those skills coupled together, they just result in massive, massive wins. And those of you who are familiar with the old Boston box or the growth share matrix, I don't want to confuse anybody. It's not all that important for our purposes. But shout out to Bruce Henderson who's passed away, but he founded the concept of functioning in higher growth markets and trying to move into leadership and staying away from particular markets that are actually on the downward trend, especially if you can't use yourself move into leadership. And we're also going to be looking at ads, because even though we're all kick-ass copywriters right now, we understand the importance of copy and we've been looking at sales, scripts and analyzing and trying to work out the best way to convert them into awesome sales pictures and sales letters and awesome headlines and ads and so forth. And we've been tracing and copying these highest performing sales letters that we've all gone through in our copywriting module still does not hurt to look at what's working for our competitors. So we're going to look at our competitors' sites. We're going to look at our competitors' ads. We're going to look at their display ads, their search ads. We're going to find out everything that they're doing that's working for them. So why not piggyback off their success if they're doing something that we know is working? Why not piggyback off that success if they're doing something that fails? Why don't we let them fail and not have to follow suit? It's far riskier to be the person who comes in with a brand new idea or decides to test a new traffic channel than it is of course to just sit back, watch what the competitors are doing and function as a counter rather than being an innovator. You can just kind of follow suit and find out and apply what's working for them to your own business and of course disregard the things that are not working for them especially if you're looking at a market leader and nine times out of ten a market leader has far more money than we do to spend and test things and they can obviously absorb a bad traffic source or a bad traffic channel or a series of bad ads or whatever whereas guys like us that might be enough to wipe us so we don't want to spend too much money testing although I love testing especially I love split testing, split testing your funnel pages you guys know how to do that or at least know where to go to get more information on the process but why would we test so aggressively when a lot of the testing has been done for us and that's what we're going to be covering in step four which is identifying the best traffic source and ads. So we're not talking about raw clicks these these visits are just these raw clicks they call it visits and these are our uniques which is the true figure okay so 16.6 million people in in a 30 day period in one month a lot of traffic there. customer now what we need to do is find our core product now depending on the case of course because this is a medical professional not everybody is suited for a root canal but if they are suited for a root canal and there is significant margin in it it may make sense for the dentist to actually put them through the process of getting a root canal right or or introducing to them the benefits of getting a root canal now if the root canal is not fitting because again this is a kind of prospect where we shouldn't be selling them something they don't need because of of of various reasons ethics not the least of which maybe a veneer may be more appropriate right the veneer is for the more the more aesthetically focused patients may want a veneer and there is a significant search volume for veneer so maybe that can be our core product offering right so what wouldn't we include in our funnel what wouldn't we try to sell to the prospect well you wouldn't want to be selling a crown not many people are interested in crowns for whatever reason implants have a low search volume as well okay so that gives us a good idea of what we should include in our sales funnel so if we were a dentist we would say okay i would certainly try to sell a teeth whining everybody's interested in teeth whining i'll put that say in the uh the first step or the second step of my sales funnel and then i'll see whether or not a root canal is suitable because i know a lot of people need root canals because a lot of people are looking for root canals so i'll definitely take a look at root canals and be mindful of whether or not this particular patient is a good candidate for a root canal and i'll also look at veneers because they have a pretty high search volume and dental implants have a reasonable high search volume although the words implant costs was it that had a lot or it was crown that had a low search volume so i'd stay away from crowns unless they're absolutely necessary because i know patients or my prospects don't really want crowns and no matter what you do so this is a very difficult example because we're talking about an um offline business and we're talking about one that's um uh that is largely constrained by um by not only legal factors but also by you know ethics and and integrity we certainly don't want to be selling people dental products who don't need the dental services excuse me who don't need that particular surgery so it was a challenging one but even with this challenging example we were able to craft to put together a pretty decent funnel based on search volume for the things that your prospects are actually looking for the things your prospects actually want and if you continue to do this you'll be able to craft or develop a sales funnel that you that almost sells itself because people are actually looking for it so as long as it's designed appropriately all you have to do is put it in front of them and they're going to buy you know you're going to get a lot of takers here for your knowledge guys it's just the average monthly searches so you know you have 38 000 here it's it's kind of trending upwards then almost trending downwards and going to really want because it's the kind of products they're actually searching for is to use merchant word now merchant words and is an analysis tool and looks into amazon so um let me just log in and we can take a look at the interface all right so what merchant words it does for us it lets us know exactly what our customers are shopping for so this is particularly useful for e-commerce clients but even if you're not in e-commerce business and you want to sell your customers something that you know that they want you can add a physical product to your funnel very easily now let's think of something to write let's say we're going to be in a weight loss market so you might be a personal trainer or a fitness coach or an executive coach or whatever and you know that your customers or clients want to lose weight so we can just type weight loss into merchant words and here we will have a look at all the kinds of um search items or search queries that your customers or prospects are indeed searching for that search by the highest search volume now weight loss pills look at that 3.7 million people are searching for weight loss pills on amazon every single month so if you are in the personal training business you may want to consider including some weight loss pills as part of your sales funnel right and you may also want to consider including strongly consider including weight loss shakes because shakes are a big seller as is weight loss tea so we know what your customers and clients want they really want the pills they really want the shakes they really want the tea i'm sure you've got the programs down pat if you're actually a personal trainer but this is a good sign 3.1 million people are actually searching for weight loss programs weight loss belt whatever the hell that is you want to search what the hell the weight loss belt is if you don't already know and if you are a personal trainer i don't doubt that you know what a weight loss belt is but you want to search it and you may want to seriously consider including that in your sales funnel so you may want to for instance send traffic to an opt-in page that teaches people uh free tips on how to lose weight so free program on how to lose weight that can be your opt-in and and then you can use your self-liquidating offer could be a weight loss book we know a lot of people want weight loss books it could be you know a free way a series of weight loss tips for people actually searching in amazon for weight loss tips they're prepared to pay money for somebody who's prepared to share some weight loss tips so you could do a self-liquidating offer with weight loss tips you could come in and do your core offer with your actual training you can train them you know at your place or at their place or at the gym or whatever it is you would do as a personal trainer and then you can do a series of upsells selling weight loss pills to your clients because we know your clients want weight loss pills and you could sell them weight loss shakes as an upsell and maybe if they say no to both of them or they're not really interested in pills and shakes right now you may present them with the downsell of weight loss teas you know and explain the benefits of weight loss tea instead of weight loss pills and shakes so you guys can see how we're forming a sales funnel now looking at real data looking what people are actually looking for and we know our customers and clients are going to want it because they're searching for it and they're searching for it actively and the good thing about merchant words is it's actually pulling out real data from amazon so this is exactly what people are going on to amazon searching for and the good thing about amazon as opposed to google is that people who are searching on amazon are always searching to buy something they are buyers no one goes on to amazon to try to get some free content like you would on google you might look for some weight loss information on google but your objective as a searcher is to try to aggregate or collect as much free information as possible so you don't need to you're not necessarily in that buying mentality right so but amazon viewers and people who are on amazon are there to buy they're there to buy they're there to shop they're not there to look for free content because frankly amazon doesn't offer any free content amazon doesn't have any free articles or blogs for the particular visitor to have a look at so this is exactly what your buyers want merchant words will tell you exactly what they want include them in your sales funnel i guarantee you're going to have some kick ass conversions and of course you as a personal trainer in this particular example who is offering their personal training service but on top of that they're also offering you know the weight loss pills the supplements the the the tea the weight loss belts right the weight loss belt you may include that into your funnel somewhere
